 {"id":"bd-3drg","title":"[IDEA-08] Retry Taxonomy + FCP Error Mapping","description":"# Retry Taxonomy + FCP Error Mapping

## Goal
Standardize retryability classification and mapping to FCP error taxonomy across connectors.

## Background / Why
Discord and Telegram each implement custom retry classifiers. Consolidating into SDK avoids divergence and improves consistency (Retry-After, backoff with jitter, terminal errors).

## Scope
- SDK-level `RetryDecision` types.
- Helper to map external errors into FCP error codes + retry hints.

## Deliverables
- `RetryDecision` enum (Immediate, Backoff, After(Duration), Terminal).
- `RetryPolicy` helper that translates `RetryDecision` into backoff and logs.
- Error mapping guidance in docs.

## Tests
- Unit tests for mapping correctness.
- Integration test with simulated HTTP errors (429/5xx/parse errors).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Connectors can classify errors without ad-hoc string matching.
- Retry behavior is consistent across connectors.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:35:45.362878825Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T01:11:48.319677117Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T01:11:48.319586739Z","close_reason":"All child tasks (bd-1kt1, bd-qxnw, bd-33qn) completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3drg","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:36:21.618496408Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- Resolution clears fork state.

- Validate with `fcp_conformance::schemas::validate_e2e_log_jsonl` (use `fcp-testkit` LogCapture in unit tests).

 {"id":"bd-3m2a","title":"TEST-CONNECTORS-LIVE: Real-service integration suites (manual)","description":"# TEST-CONNECTORS-LIVE: Real-service integration suites (manual)

## Goal
Add live integration tests for external services without mocks/fakes, using dedicated sandbox accounts.

## Scope
- Anthropic, OpenAI, Telegram, Discord, Twitter connectors.
- Real API calls with rate limits and secret handling.

## Constraints
- Must run as manual-only or gated by secrets in CI.
- No production accounts, no recording or fake fixtures.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each connector has a live test plan and minimal smoke tests.
- Logs redact secrets and include correlation IDs and cost/latency metadata.

 {"id":"bd-3p99","title":"TEST: No-mock coverage + full E2E logging (FCP2)","description":"# TEST: No-mock coverage + full E2E logging (FCP2)

## Goal
Establish real-component unit/integration coverage (no mocks/fakes) plus a complete E2E script suite with rich structured logging.

## Definition: No mocks/fakes
- No mocking frameworks (wiremock), stub servers, or fake clients in the target suites.
- Tests exercise real code paths (actual protocol parsing, crypto, stores, mesh/session logic, sandbox enforcement).
- For external services (LLM, social APIs), tests must use dedicated sandbox accounts and real HTTP calls OR be marked manual-only with secrets gating.

## Deliverables
- Coverage inventory + gap map.
- Real-component test suites for core crates, mesh, store/repair, host+connector subprocess, sandbox/egress.
- E2E logging schema + validator + expanded scenario runner.

## Non-goals
- Do not delete existing mock-based tests; instead add real-component suites in parallel.
- No reliance on public production accounts.

## Success Criteria
- A coherent dependency graph of tasks that yields: (1) real-component unit/integration coverage, (2) comprehensive E2E scripts with machine-parseable logs.

 {"id":"bd-3pdj","title":"[IDEA-19] Structured Log Redaction + Secret Scanner","description":"## Background
Logging standards forbid secrets/PII, but enforcement is inconsistent. A mechanical scanner reduces risk and enforces compliance across tests and CI artifacts.

## Goals
- Provide a reusable log scanner that detects secrets/PII patterns in JSONL logs.
- Integrate with test harnesses and compliance runner.
- Allow explicit allowlists for safe test fixtures.

## Non-goals
- Replacing runtime redaction; this is a verification layer.

## Approach
- Add a `LogRedactionScanner` in `fcp-testkit` or `fcp-conformance`.
- Detect common secret formats (JWTs, API keys, auth headers, base64 blobs) and obvious PII.
- Expose CLI for scanning artifacts.

## Risks
- False positives; provide explicit per-test allowlists and explainable matches.

## Success Criteria
- CI fails if secrets appear in logs.
- Scanner provides actionable match locations and suggestions.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Scanner detects JWTs, bearer tokens, and API key patterns in JSONL.
- Allowlist requires explicit justification string.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.1.2","title":"fcp.telegram: Provisioning Automation (Bot setup)","description":"# fcp.telegram: Provisioning Automation (Bot setup)

## Goal
Make Telegram setup **automatic and low-friction** while remaining security-first and zone-scoped.

## What must be automated
- Validate that a provided bot token is syntactically valid and works (via a safe read-only call).
- Surface minimal user prompts (only what cannot be inferred).

## Provisioning interface
Use the platform AutomationRecipe/Provisioning interface:
- “I need a bot token” is modeled as a `CredentialId` requirement.
- The recipe verifies token correctness without leaking it.

## Optional: webhook mode (later)
If/when we support Telegram webhooks:
- Connector must NOT expose an inbound port directly.
- `fcp-host` owns the public endpoint; it forwards verified webhook payloads over FCPC.
- Enabling/disabling webhooks is a **Dangerous** action:
  - requires `telegram.webhooks.manage`
  - requires ApprovalToken `Execution` scope
  - emits AuditEvents

## UX requirements
- Clear errors for common mistakes:
  - wrong token
  - bot blocked by chat
  - insufficient permissions
- A “doctor” check path that validates:
  - token valid
  - network constraints allow Telegram
  - rate-limit config sane

## Acceptance Criteria
- Provisioning flow can bring a new Telegram bot online with minimal steps.
- All verification is mockable for unit tests.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.1.3","title":"fcp.telegram: Polling + Event Streaming (getUpdates)","description":"# fcp.telegram: Polling + Event Streaming (getUpdates)

## Goal
Implement Telegram inbound updates as an FCP2 streaming source:
- consume updates from Telegram Bot API (`getUpdates` long-poll)
- emit FCP2 `EventEnvelope`s with correct topics, taints, and cursors

## Core requirements
### Singleton-writer
- The poll loop MUST be protected by a singleton-writer lease.
- Non-lease-holder instances must fail closed (no polling).

### Cursor model
- Persist `update_id` cursor in connector state.
- Ensure restart resumes without re-emitting old updates.

### Event emission
- Map Telegram update types → event topics.
- All inbound content must be tainted as external input.
- Attach sufficient provenance (chat id, message id, update id) without leaking secrets.

### Backpressure + bounds
- Bound poll concurrency and queue sizes.
- No unbounded memory growth on high-volume chats.

### Timeouts
- Long poll timeout is bounded and policy-controlled.
- Cancellation/shutdown must be prompt.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic integration tests can script an update stream and observe emitted EventEnvelopes.
- Replay/ack behavior is consistent with the connector SDK’s streaming contract.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.1.4","title":"fcp.telegram: Send + Media Operations (receipts, fail-closed)","description":"# fcp.telegram: Send + Media Operations (receipts, fail-closed)

## Goal
Implement the side-effecting operation surface for Telegram in a way that is:
- capability-gated
- auditable
- safe under retries and partial failures

## Operations (initial)
- `telegram.send_message`
- `telegram.send_media`

## Exactly-once / retry safety
Telegram does not provide robust server-side idempotency keys.

Design requirement:
- If the connector cannot prove whether a send succeeded (e.g., timeout after request was sent), it MUST **fail closed** to avoid accidental duplicate sends.
- When a send succeeds and returns a definitive `message_id`, bind that to an OperationReceipt.

## Audit requirements
- Emit AuditEvents for each side-effecting send (redacting message bodies by default).
- Include correlation IDs that allow tracing user intent → invoke → receipt.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Conformance runner can validate default-deny and that allowed calls produce receipts.
- Integration tests cover timeout/partial failure behavior.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.1.6","title":"E2E: Telegram Connector Compliance Run","description":"# E2E: Telegram Connector Compliance Run

## Goal
Add Telegram-specific scenarios to the shared FCP2 connector compliance runner and system E2E harness.

This is where we prove:
- protocol correctness end-to-end (spawn connector → invoke → receipts)
- sandbox enforcement (network allow/deny)
- taint/provenance propagation

## Scenarios
- Default deny (no token): denial + DecisionReceipt reason_code must match spec expectations.
- Allow (valid token): invoke succeeds, receipt emitted.
- Network guard deny: attempt any non-telegram host → deny at Network Guard boundary.
- Streaming: emit events from scripted updates and validate cursors + acks/replay.

## Logging
- Structured JSON logs and a summarized report artifact (machine-readable).

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo test --package fcp-e2e --features telegram` passes deterministically.
- Failure output is actionable (reason_code + evidence surfaced).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.10","title":"fcp.box — Enterprise File Storage Connector","description":"# fcp.box — Enterprise File Storage Connector

## Goal
Provide a V2-compliant connector for Box (enterprise file storage): browse/search files, download/upload, and share links under strict zone/capability control.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)
- `storage`
- `operational`
- `streaming`

> Interaction patterns (non-archetype): File/Blob + Request/Response (+ optional webhooks)

## FCP2 Notes
- Single-zone binding + default deny.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to Box API hosts.
- Large file transfers must be bounded and use chunked object patterns (ChunkedObjectManifest) where applicable.

## Success Criteria
- Before implementation begins, create the base beads `.1`..`.4` per `flywheel_connectors-lszk.5` (manifest, provisioning, tests, E2E).
- Passes `flywheel_connectors-1n78.21.5` (mechanical compliance) and `flywheel_connectors-e3i9` (E2E evidence bundle).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.10.1","title":"fcp.box: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"# fcp.box: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)

## Goal
Define the complete FCP2 manifest surface for fcp.box:
- operation list + schema IDs
- capability families (per operation)
- sandbox profile requirements
- NetworkConstraints (deny-by-default)
- state model declaration (stateless/singleton_writer/crdt)

## Notes
- Capability IDs MUST NOT encode hostnames/ports.
- If streaming/webhooks exist, replay/ack/cursor semantics must be explicitly declared.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Manifest validates strictly.
- Mechanical compliance runner accepts the manifest.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.2.2","title":"fcp.discord: Provisioning Automation (Bot + intents)","description":"# fcp.discord: Provisioning Automation (Bot + intents)

## Goal
Make Discord bot onboarding as automatic as possible:
- validate bot token
- validate required gateway intents for declared event topics
- validate that NetworkConstraints allow Discord endpoints

## Provisioning interface
Use AutomationRecipe/Provisioning:
- secrets: bot token (`CredentialId`)
- parameters: guild/channel allowlist (if policy requires)

## Safety requirements
- Connector must not “self-escalate” privileges.
- Anything that changes server configuration (webhooks/channel mgmt) is Dangerous and requires ApprovalToken `Execution` scope.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `fcp doctor` can confirm readiness: token ok, intents ok, network ok.
- Entire flow is mockable for unit tests.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.2.3","title":"fcp.discord: Gateway Streaming (events → EventEnvelope)","description":"# fcp.discord: Gateway Streaming (events → EventEnvelope)

## Goal
Implement Discord Gateway connectivity as an FCP2 streaming source:
- connect to gateway
- consume events
- emit EventEnvelopes with correct topics, taints, and cursors

## Requirements
- Singleton-writer lease around the gateway connection.
- Persist sequence/session state to support safe resume.
- Backpressure: bound event buffering.
- Strict parsing: malformed frames must never panic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic integration tests can drive a fake gateway and observe emitted EventEnvelopes.
- Resume behavior is correct (or fails closed when unsafe).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.2.4","title":"fcp.discord: REST Operations (messages/reactions/channels)","description":"# fcp.discord: REST Operations (messages/reactions/channels)

## Goal
Implement the initial Discord REST operation slice safely:
- `discord.send_message`
- `discord.add_reaction`
- `discord.list_channels`
- `discord.create_thread`

## Requirements
- Capability gating is mechanical.
- Rate limiting is bucket-aware and obeys Retry-After.
- Side effects are retry-safe:
  - prefer fail-closed on uncertain outcomes
  - optionally use `nonce` to dedupe message sends

## Acceptance Criteria
- Integration tests cover success + 401/403/429 + timeout.
- AuditEvents emitted for side-effecting actions.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.2.6","title":"E2E: Discord Connector Compliance Run","description":"# E2E: Discord Connector Compliance Run

## Goal
Add Discord-specific scenarios to the shared connector compliance runner and system E2E harness.

## Scenarios
- Default deny.
- Allow with valid token.
- Network guard allow/deny (block any non-discord host).
- Gateway streaming + cursor behavior.
- Rate limit behavior under scripted load.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo test --package fcp-e2e --features discord` passes deterministically.
- Logs and report artifacts are machine-readable and actionable.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.20","title":"fcp.duckdb — Analytical Database Connector","description":"# fcp.duckdb — Analytical Database Connector

## Goal
Provide a V2-compliant connector for DuckDB.

Likely patterns:
- local embedded DB per zone with strict filesystem sandboxing
- explicit import/export of data as mesh objects

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)
- `storage`
- `operational`

> Interaction patterns (non-archetype): Embedded DB + Request/Response queries

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints` (defined in `.1`).
- Allow only the configured API/base host(s); HTTPS-only; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.
- For local-first connectors, `NetworkConstraints` should be empty/deny-all unless an explicit sync feature is enabled.

## FCP2 Notes
- Single-zone binding + default deny.
- Filesystem access must be explicitly scoped and audited.

## Success Criteria
- Before implementation begins, create the base beads `.1`..`.4` per `flywheel_connectors-lszk.5`.
